page.wizardBannerDesc: Design an AI solution yourself and get started with a ZIP download
page.modernizeBannerDesc: Connect a GitHub repository and AI will diagnose and automatically register modernization tasks in Linear

detail.staleKeyDesc: Your local .env file needs to be updated. {keys} key(s) are outdated.
detail.noWizardData: No wizard configuration available. Run the solution wizard again to regenerate the ZIP.
detail.dangerZoneDesc: Actions below cannot be undone. Proceed with caution.
detail.resetDesc: Deletes wizard configuration, tickets, artifacts, and session data. Project ID and name are preserved.
detail.deleteDesc: Permanently deletes the project and all associated data.
detail.resetKeyUpdateNote: Update LICENSE_KEY in your local .env file with the new key above.

linear.credentialsNotSavedDesc: Enter Linear API key and team ID when creating a project to auto-save
linear.savedNote: Linear credentials are saved for this project.
linear.notSavedNote: Enter your Linear API key in the project creation wizard to save it for this project.

members.title: Organization Members
members.subtitle: Invite and manage organization members
members.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove?
members.emptyStateHint: Use the 'Invite member' button above to add members
members.noOrg: You don't belong to any organization

linear.title: Linear Integration
linear.subtitle: AI Team tasks are automatically registered as Linear issues, and local Claude runs automatically when status changes.
linear.apiKeyHelp: Issued from Linear → Settings → API → Personal API keys
linear.teamIdHelp: Linear → Settings → Workspace → Teams → Click the team name → Copy ID
linear.tunnelUrlHelp: URL output after running setup-tunnel.sh in the ZIP
linear.webhookPlaceholderSet: Set - enter a new value to change
linear.saveSuccess_webhook: Saved. Linear Webhook has been automatically registered.

guide.tunnelTitle: How do I get a tunnel URL?
guide.tunnelDesc: The tunnel URL is an external address that makes your local webhook server accessible from the internet. It is automatically configured when you run bash start.sh.
guide.tunnelStep1Title: Run launcher after unzipping (tunnel & webhook auto-configured)
guide.tunnelStep2Paste: Paste the output URL into the input field below.
guide.tunnelStep3Title: When URL refresh is needed (after WSL2 restart)
guide.tunnelStep3Note: Re-running will automatically re-register the webhook with the new URL.
guide.tunnelWarning: Free Cloudflare tunnels change URL on WSL2 restart. Run bash start.sh again after restart. Registering as a systemd service (install-service.sh) keeps the webhook server and watcher running automatically.
guide.webhookDesc: A random string used for signature verification when Linear sends events to your local server. Optional but recommended for security.
guide.webhookGenerateTitle: Generate a random secret in terminal:
guide.webhookPasteDesc: Copy the output and enter it in both the input field below and WEBHOOK_SECRET in the ZIP's .env file.
guide.webhookNote: When you register a tunnel URL, ClickEye automatically registers a Linear webhook. The secret you enter here is applied to that webhook.

anthropic.title: Anthropic Credentials
anthropic.subtitle: Register the credentials required for your Claude authentication method. The appropriate credentials for the mode selected in the wizard will be automatically injected when generating a ZIP.
anthropic.apiKeyCardDescription: Used when 'API Key' mode is selected in the wizard. It is automatically injected into the ZIP's .env as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
anthropic.apiKeyInvalid: Invalid format (must start with sk-ant-... and be at least 20 characters)
anthropic.apiKeyExternalLink: Issue from console.anthropic.com → API Keys (sk-ant-... format)
anthropic.apiKeyHelper: The key is stored on the server with Fernet encryption. When analyzing solution blueprints in the wizard, this key is used first and credits from your own account are consumed before the server key.
anthropic.oauthNoRegistrationDesc: When 'OAuth Browser' mode is selected in the wizard, no credentials are stored in the ClickEye cloud.
anthropic.oauthBullet1: When you run bash start.sh, claude login proceeds automatically.
anthropic.oauthBullet2: The auth token is only stored in the OS keychain (~/.claude.json) and is not sent to ClickEye.
anthropic.oauthBullet3: There is nothing to manage on this page for this mode. Select your auth method in the wizard and download the ZIP.

guide.title: User Guide
guide.subtitle: Step-by-step guide to using the ClickEye platform.

login.title: Welcome back
login.subtitle: Log in to the console to run your deliveries

register.title: Create your team account

register.termsPrefix: By signing up, you agree to our

layout.heroDescLine1: Break each engagement into Linear issues and let AI runners build them in parallel.
layout.heroDescLine2: Governance gates and a cost ledger keep quality and margin in check.

console.title: Delivery console
console.desc: Every active engagement on one screen

governance.title: Governance gate
governance.desc: Safe auto-merge via validation and risk classification

hybrid.title: Hybrid execution
hybrid.desc: Local subscription sessions first, cloud fallback

hero.desc: Break each engagement into Linear issues, let AI runners build them in parallel, and keep quality and margin in check with governance gates and a cost ledger. Your team focuses on control, not execution.

board.caption: delivery-console
board.note: Example — populated with your real delivery data

process.sectionTitle: How one engagement flows through the control plane
process.sectionDescription: A real delivery pipeline in four connected stages.

intake.title: Contract intake
intake.desc: Register an engagement and its scope is broken into Linear issues with roles assigned.

delivery.title: AI parallel delivery
delivery.desc: Hybrid runners prefer local subscription sessions and fall back to the cloud, building many issues at once.

governance.title: Governance gate
governance.desc: Contracts, tickets and plans are validated and risk-classified so only safe changes merge automatically.

control.title: Control & cost
control.desc: Watch pipelines, reviews and token usage in real time, and track per-project margin with a cost ledger.

capabilities.sectionTitle: Control tooling for running many SI projects at once
capabilities.sectionDescription: Automate execution, keep control with the team.

console.title: Delivery console
console.desc: Watch the pipeline stage and issue status of every active engagement on one screen.

governance.title: Governance SSOT
governance.desc: Manage validation and risk rules from a single source so every merge meets the same bar.

cost.title: Cost visibility
cost.desc: Aggregate tokens and run cost per issue to surface project margin in real time.

temporal.title: Temporal orchestration
temporal.desc: Durably orchestrate long-running delivery workflows and resume safely from the point of failure.

hybrid.title: Hybrid execution
hybrid.desc: Prefer local subscription sessions and auto-distribute to cloud runners under load.

linear.title: Linear integration
linear.desc: Break engagements into issues and trigger runners automatically as statuses change.

finalCta.title: Execution to the AI, control to your team.
finalCta.description: Open the console, register your first engagement and start parallel delivery.

governance.title: Governance Policy
governance.riskDemote: High-risk changes are demoted to a PR instead of a direct merge.

console.subtitle: SI delivery console
console.projectError: Failed to load engagement info. Showing by ID.
console.noSessions: This engagement has no work sessions yet
console.summaryError: Some data could not be refreshed.
console.scopeCurrent: MVP-1·2 Issue board · Pipeline · Review · Cost (implemented now)
console.scopeFuture: Gray = Phase 2/3 (gate decision history · per-issue cost · Temporal lookup — backend first)

cost.title: AI execution cost
cost.emptyDesc: Tokens and cost will be aggregated here as work proceeds.
cost.restrictedDesc: Ask an administrator for the settings:manage permission.
cost.footer: In-house development uses subscription sessions first. The headline metric is tokens; cost is calculated only for organization API key usage.

mock.label: Mock data
mock.banner: Showing mock data — not real data.

chat.title: Delivery Assistant
chat.unavailable: LLM assistant unavailable — the llm profile may not be running.
chat.error: An error occurred while generating the answer.
chat.hint: Answers are grounded in accumulated delivery knowledge. Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline.
chat.mockDesc: The LLM assistant is available on real deliveries.

feedback.thanks: Thanks for your feedback. It helps improve answer quality.

orgChat.title: Org Assistant
orgChat.subtitle: Ask about active deliveries across your organization
orgChat.placeholder: e.g., What services are we producing right now?
orgChat.hint: Answers are grounded in your organization's active deliveries. Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline.

intake.pageDescription: Review and accept or reject requirements submitted by external services
intake.featureDisabled: The intake feature is disabled (FEATURE_INTAKE). Enable it in the backend settings to see submissions.

refine.title: Refined spec
refine.acceptNotice: The project will be created from the refined spec.

acceptDialog.title: Accept intake
acceptDialog.description: Accepting "{title}" will create a project. Continue?

keys.description: Issue and manage machine keys external services use to submit intakes. The plaintext key is shown only once right after issuance.
keys.createdWarning: This plaintext key is shown only once. It cannot be viewed again after closing this window, so store it somewhere safe.
keys.deactivateDescription: Once deactivated, this key can no longer submit intakes. The record is kept for auditing.

containers.pageDescription: View running container status and service port reachability. Read-only.

env.pageDescription: Edit the API service's managed environment variables. After changes, click Apply to see the regeneration command; an operator must run it manually to take effect. (Build-time vars like NEXT_PUBLIC_* are out of scope.)
env.deleteDescription: Delete the {key} environment variable. It takes effect after running the regeneration command.

render.title: Regeneration Command
render.manualNotice: The server does not run this command automatically. Copy the command below and run it manually in your ops environment for changes to take effect.
render.error: Failed to generate the regeneration command.

tables.pageDescription: Only whitelisted tables can be viewed and edited.
tables.deleteDescription: Delete row {pk}. This action cannot be undone.
tables.sensitiveKeepHint: Enter only to change (leave blank to keep)
